english: sríṅ (srin), _og ('og) klu-gñyán (klu gnyan), bar (bar) gźi-bdág (gzhi bdag), are often mentioned in connexion. — lha-srúṅ (lha srung) tutelar god, bód-kyi (bod kyi) Glr.
knee-pan, pús-mo gyás-pai lha-ṅá sá-la _dzúg-pa (pus mo g.yas pa'i lha nga sa la 'dzug pa) to kneel down on the right knee frq.; lha-kúṅ (lha khung) the bend of the knee W.
Med. and Pth., acc. to Wdn. also klad-rgyás (klad rgyas), Sch.: 'the bloody marrow in the bones; whilst he translates rmai lhá-ba (rma'i lha ba) by: 'the growing worse of a wound'.
Med.; Sch. cartilage (cf. lha-gór (lha gor) sub lkóg-ma (lkog ma)).

1. more, beyond, *dá-wa čig lag soṅ* (zla ba cig lag song) more than a month has passed W., *nyi-ma-pyéd lag soṅ* (nyi ma phyed lag song) W. it is already past noon, rgyá-mtśo-bas kyaṅ lhág-ste (rgya mtsho bas kyang lhag ste) as this alone would be more than the whole sea Dzl.; nyi-śus lhag (nyi shus lhag) more by twenty S.g. — 2. = lhág-ma (lhag ma). — lhág-pa (lhag pa) 1. adj. surpassing, excelling, superior, _dí-las lhág-pai gźan čos med ('di las lhag pa'i gzhan chos med) Dzl.; nyam-čhúṅ-ba ṅá-las lhág-pa yód-dam (nyam chung ba nga las lhag pa yod dam) is there any one that is thinner than I? Dzl.; dé-rnams-kyi náṅ-nas lhág-pa (de rnams kyi nang nas lhag pa) the best amongst these Glr.; rgya hor gnyís-las lhág-pa ṅed bod yín (rgya hor gnyis las lhag pa nged bod yin) we Tibetans surpass the Chinese and Mongols (in sagacity) Glr. 2. rarely = lhág-ma (lhag ma): dé-las lhág-pa (de las lhag pa) the others, the remaining S.g. 6, 1. de-lhag (de lhag) besides, moreover. — lhág-par (lhag par) adv. more (magis) frq., mostly with adj., but also with verbs, śin-tu lhag-par (shin tu lhag par) far more; very, exceedingly, uncommonly, lhág-par légs-pa (lhag par legs pa) uncommonly beautiful Dzl.; čes lhág-par (ches lhag par) extremely, excessively Stg.; further, furthermore, moreover. — lhág-ge-ba (lhag ge ba) = lhag-pa (lhag pa). lhág-ma (lhag ma) 1. remains, scrapings, sṅar bśad-pai lhág-ma (sngar bshad pa'i lhag ma) (the letters) which at the last discussion remained, were left, (unexplained) Gram.; lha lhág-ma-rnams (lha lhag ma rnams) the other, the remaining gods Stg.; the remainder, in subtracting Wdk. — 2. razor-stone, razor-strap Sch. — 3. being above, being at the upper part of, groṅ (grong) the place lying higher up (the valley), opp. to _óg-ma ('og ma) Dzl. Comp. lhag-bčas (lhag bcas) 1. having more than so and so much, bha-ra-ta ślo-ka _bum lhag-bčas (bha ra ta shlo ka 'bum lhag bcas) the Bharata of more than 100,000 verses Tar. 2. the gerund in te (ste etc.) (te (ste etc.)) Gram. — lhag-mtóṅ (lhag mthong) 'seeing more' (than other mortals), in certain states of contemplation v. źi-gnas (zhi gnas) sub źi (zhi).
= lhaṅ-ṅer (lhang nger), lhag-gis _čar (lhag gis 'char) Thgr., _byuṅ ('byung) Mil. he shines bright, lhág-ge-ba (lhag ge ba) = lhaṅ-ṅe-ba (lhang nge ba) Mil.
I. sbst., also gza lhág-pa (gza' lhag pa) 1. Mercury. 2. Wednesday. — II. adj. more excellent, v. lhag (lhag).

I. sbst. cold wind, lhág-pa póg (lhag pa phog) the cold wind has withered them (the flowers) W.; lo-gsár lhags-pa (lo gsar lhags pa) the cold new year's wind Mil. — II. vb., to come together, to meet, to assemble, with other persons; perh. also: to join, to be adjacent or contiguous, of houses, beams etc.

lhaṅ-ṅe (lhang nge), lhan-ṅe (lhan nge), lham-me (lham me), also lhag-ge (lhag ge), lam-me (lam me), lham-pa (lham pa) clear, distinct, to the sight as well as to the ear; lhaṅ-ṅe lham-mer gsal-te (lhang nge lham mer gsal te) (Buddha) appearing clearly and distinctly Dzl., in a similar sense: lham-me lhaṅ-ṅe lhaṅ-ṅer bźúgs-so (lham me lhang nge lhang nger bzhugs so) Pth.; clear, sonorous; kyí-skad lhaṅ-lhaṅ-pa (khyi skad lhang lhang pa) Mil. the clear (loud) barking of dogs; kyoḍ lhaṅ-lhaṅ glú-len-pa (khyod lhang lhang glu len pa) Mil. thou clear-voiced songster; lhaṅ-lhúṅ brjód-la (lhang lhung brjod la) speaking with a clear, sonorous voice. — Cs. has: majestic, glorious, sublime, august.

a baser substance mixed with a finer one, an alloy, lhad _júg-pa (lhad 'jug pa) or sré-ba (sre ba) with la (la), lhád-kyis slád-pa (lhad kyis slad pa) with accus. Mil., to alloy, adulterate, lhád-čan (lhad can) adulterated, e.g. milk C., lhad-méd (lhad med) unadulterated, pure, genuine, real; *tsig hlẹ' śor* (tshig lhad shor) C. or *źug soṅ* (zhug song) W. spurious words are admixed, falsehoods have been artfully introduced; pyis lhád-du bcúg-pai tsig (phyis lhad du bcug pa'i tshig) Tar. a later interpolation. — 2. Bal., *ylad* (glad), fatigued, exhausted.

together, lhán-gyis (lhan gyis) (when referring to the subject of the action), lhán-nas (lhan nas) (as ablative case), lhán-du (lhan du) (the most frq. form) with one another, together, lhan-čig (-tu) (lhan cig (-tu)), and often lhan-číg(-tu) (lhan cig (-tu)) id.: bdag daṅ lhan-číg zan mi zá-na (bdag dang lhan cig zan mi za na) if you will not eat together with me Dzl., rta bčus lhán-du rgyúgs-pa ltá-bui sgra (rta bcus lhan du rgyugs pa lta bu'i sgra) a noise as if ten
